The logical deduction instantly turns into an extremely absurd comedy: in a row of office rooms on the 7th floor of a high-tech park, the boss hasn't hired a single employee, holds 400,000 in capital, and claims to have built an 'offshore oil platform and shipyard'? This is the most direct insult to 10,000-ton level heavy industry. The probe mercilessly deleted all the 'manufacturing' delusions in the license, extracting the only work it can do in the 7th-floor offices: 'marine equipment repair, maintenance services; parts wholesale and retail; import and export business.' The boss utilized industry connections, spent 400k to set up a shell, purely to flip ship parts like anchor windlasses and cranes to pocket the margin, or to secure ship repair jobs and outsource them to dock workers.
